title: UPS Haslet robotic system not responding; AGVs out of sync after OptiSweep service crash

site.notes: Case metadata and CBRE/work-order evidence point to Haslet/Fort Worth TXRTH site, while one RMS screenshot artifact notes an apparent Alliance, TX timestamp/location string. Preserve ambiguity pending SME review.

kpi_tracking.basis: Use CBRE/work-order entry time as the source-supported tracking start and the Salesforce close comment stating the site is running as the tracking end.

source_refs.quote_or_summary: CBRE SERVICEInsight work order entered 2/20/2026 7:19 AM for the robotic system not responding.
Salesforce close comment at 2:58 PM on 2026-02-20 states that the site is running and the case will be closed.

notes: The close timestamp is user-provided review evidence from a Salesforce comment screenshot.
The tracking start is the current best extracted source-supported start time, not necessarily the first physical failure moment.

symptoms: Nothing was moving at the site.
Robotic system was reported as not responding.
Multiple bots/AGVs were not moving and AGVs were reported out of sync.
RMS/HMI evidence showed an abnormal blank state with '.NET Control not created.'
Application and service errors were recorded around 2026-02-20 06:35-06:36, including WCSWebApplication errors and an OptiSweep service termination.
incident_description: High-priority production incident at the UPS Haslet/Fort Worth site where the robotic system stopped responding and site movement halted. Source evidence shows OptiSweep service termination at about 06:36, AGVs going out of sync, abnormal RMS/HMI state, and concurrent WCS web application/database-related errors. Investigation included Event Viewer review, Ignition status checks, service-state checks, API validation, and RMS review of affected AGVs.
customer_operational_impact: Production was seriously impacted. Evidence states nothing was moving, the robotic system was not responding, the small sort was not running, bots could not move in various areas, and the site had to manually bag out before resuming automated bag-out activity.
systems_involved: Windows Event Viewer / Service Control Manager
what_resolved_it: Source-supported recovery evidence says the OptiSweep service was reset after the crash, then RMS was used to locate and fix AGVs that were out of sync, after which the site resumed the auto bag-out procedure. Additional investigation noted a faulted webservice/web application associated with memory usage and log-size tracking.
follow_up_required: SME review of location ambiguity between Haslet/Fort Worth and Alliance, TX references.
SME review of whether WCS web application memory/log growth was a related contributing issue or a separate concurrent fault.
Confirm whether KPI should measure full customer downtime or case-handling duration.
Review service/application logs and Event Viewer evidence around 06:35-06:36 for final incident classification.
support_boundary: Support evidence shows remote software/application and service troubleshooting across OptiSweep, RMS, Ignition, and WCS application layers. No hardware root cause is source-confirmed. Customer/site operations impact and CBRE dispatch are documented, but root cause remains outside confirmed canonical scope.

source_refs.quote_or_summary: Case 00228086 subject: 'UPS haslet nothing is moving'; account UPS Fort Worth, TX (Haslet); priority 2 high production seriously impacted.
CBRE work order shows P1 Emergency, dispatched, robotic system not responding, and small sort not running at TXRTH - HASLET HUB.
Case comments report OptiSweep service crashed at 6:36 AM, causing AGVs out of sync and system stopped; after reset, RMS was used to fix AGVs and site resumed auto bag out.
Event 7031 Service Control Manager: OptiSweep service terminated unexpectedly; corrective action to restart service in 60000 milliseconds; logged 2/20/2026 6:36:02 AM.
Application log shows clustered WCSWebApplication and Application Error events around 6:36 AM.
WCSWebApplication Event ID 20102 shows failed database command execution around 6:35:24 AM.
Command prompt shows Ignition gateway restart initiated with 'Waiting for Gateway restart...'.
Windows Services screenshot shows OptiSweep selected and running.
API GetAgvStatuses returned HTTP 200 with visible NORMAL statuses, used as validation evidence.
RMS/HMI screenshot shows abnormal blank state with message '.NET Control not created.'
CBRE SERVICEInsight work order entered 2/20/2026 7:19 AM for the robotic system not responding.
Salesforce close comment at 2:58 PM on 2026-02-20 states that the site is running and the case will be closed.

conflicts_or_ambiguities: Location evidence is mixed: Haslet/Fort Worth/TXRTH references are strong, but one artifact notes apparent Alliance, TX text.
Evidence shows both OptiSweep service failure and WCS web application/database-related faults near the same time; causal relationship is not confirmed.
Case workflow status changes in Salesforce do not by themselves prove exact operational recovery time.
unsupported_or_missing_details: No extracted source directly confirms the exact first failure moment before the 07:19 work-order entry, though logs show failures around 06:35-06:36.
Final root cause is not source-confirmed.
Close/resolution timestamp comes from Stage 4 review evidence rather than extracted OCR/artifact content.

retrieval_text: Case 228086 UPS Haslet Fort Worth TXRTH robotic system not responding nothing is moving production seriously impacted small sort not running OptiSweep service crashed at 6:36 AM AGVs out of sync system stopped RMS .NET Control not created WCSWebApplication errors failed DbCommand Ignition gateway restart service reset RMS used to fix out-of-sync AGVs site resumed auto bag out resolved same day
